rationale: Synthetic obligation is ({antecedent}) ==> (r1 == r2) for r1, r2: Vec<CPacket>. Antecedent only ties both vectors to the same un_acked_messages(src@) via map_values(|p|p@).to_set() (and outbound_packet_seq_is_valid / outbound_packet_seq_has_correct_srcs / packets_are_valid_messages, none of which constrain ordering). Two Vec<CPacket> with the same multiset/abstract set can have different orderings, so r1@ == r2@ is not derivable; Verus correctly reports 'postcondition not satisfied'. Tried (1) an `assert(antecedent ==> equal) by { if antecedent { assert(r1@.map_values...to_set() == r2@.map_values...to_set()); assert(r1@ =~= r2@); assert(r1 == r2); } }` -- =~= step fails because Set equality does not imply Seq equality; (2) just the set-equality witness inside the by-block -- the by-block still must close the outer implication which Verus rejects. No assume/admit/external_body workaround is permitted, so the implication cannot be closed given the current spec. The determinism check is l
